2606.10972.title: Optimizing 2D Input Representations and Sub-phase Fusion Strategies for Differential Diagnosis of Asthma and COPD Using CNN- and GRU-Based Networks
2606.10972.paper_summary: This study aims to explore the performance of the VAR model in comparison with mel-frequency cepstral coefficient (MFCC) matrices and log-mel spectrograms using deep learning. In pulmonary sound classification, spectrogram-based representations suffer from inconsistent temporal dimensions due to varying respiratory cycle durations. Along with traditional trimming/zero-padding, adaptive-length windowing was presented to fix their temporal dimensions. Their spectral and temporal dimensions were optimized by testing a range of parameters. Different convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures were employed to extract features from the two-dimensional representations obtained over the sub-phases. The extracted sub-phase features were then fused using various strategies including direct concatenation, gated recurrent unit (GRU) network and GRU with attention mechanism. Model performances were assessed through respiratory cycle-based evaluation and subject-based evaluation comprising multiple respiratory cycles. Several data augmentation techniques were also studied to cope with limitations in data size. The best cycle-based F1-score (0.877) was obtained using the MFCC matrices with thirteen coefficients and 64-point time resolution per sub-phase representation followed by direct feature concatenation, and the best subject-based F1-score (0.855) was obtained using the MFCC matrices with thirteen coefficients and 256-point time resolution per full-cycle representation, both obtained by adaptive-length windowing. Augmentation degraded the performance of models overall, yet mixup augmentation was the best among the methods tested. MFCC outperformed log-mel spectrogram and VAR model in differentiation of asthma and COPD. Sophisticated fusion strategies did not improve the diagnosis. Augmentation did not contribute, demonstrating the significance of authentic data in pulmonary sound studies.
2606.10972.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究旨在探討VAR模型在與梅爾頻率倒譜係數（MFCC）矩陣和對數梅爾頻譜圖的比較中，使用深度學習的表現。在肺部聲音分類中，基於譜圖的表示因呼吸週期持續時間的變化而遭受不一致的時間維度。除了傳統的修剪/零填充外，還提出了自適應長度窗口來修正它們的時間維度。通過測試一系列參數來優化它們的頻譜和時間維度。使用了不同的卷積神經網絡（CNN）架構來從在子階段獲得的二維表示中提取特徵。然後，使用包括直接連接、門控循環單元（GRU）網絡和帶注意力機制的GRU在內的各種策略來融合提取的子階段特徵。模型的性能通過基於呼吸週期的評估和基於受試者的評估來進行評估，後者包括多個呼吸週期。還研究了幾種數據增強技術以應對數據大小的限制。使用具有十三個係數和每個子階段表示64點時間分辨率的MFCC矩陣，通過直接特徵連接獲得了最佳的基於週期的F1分數（0.877），而使用具有十三個係數和每個完整週期表示256點時間分辨率的MFCC矩陣，通過自適應長度窗口獲得了最佳的基於受試者的F1分數（0.855）。增強整體上降低了模型的性能，但mixup增強在測試的方法中是最佳的。MFCC在區分哮喘和COPD方面優於對數梅爾頻譜圖和VAR模型。複雜的融合策略並未改善診斷。增強並未做出貢獻，顯示出在肺部聲音研究中真實數據的重要性。
2606.10972.author: Ipek Sen et.al.
2606.10972.authors: Ipek Sen, Ozgur Ozdemir, Elena Battini Sonmez
